general: All mechanics requires shields! | ➔ Indicates Carry Over

Mitigation for the first Mystery Magic should carry over till the first Double-Trouble trap unless there is a different usage timing below. Targeted mitigation does not work on Wave Cannon, but does apply to Double-Trouble Trap.
Use mitigation for Light of Judgement late into the castbar so it will cover Hyperdrive.

footnotes.1: Use your 90s party mitigation as Kefka re-centres to cast the first Graven Image (WAR/PLD can use after Revolting Ruin III finishes).
footnotes.2: Use your 30s mitigation for the first Mystery Magic after the Graven Image castbar.
footnotes.3: You can alternatively use Bell just before the first set of puddles which will provide an immediate heal when the second set of puddles occurs as the Bell will expire shortly after.
footnotes.4: If you plan to use Dissipation in your opener, use it before Aetherflow. If you use the first Spreadlo earlier, you will get it back for the Double-Trouble Trap in the second Graven Image and be able to use Seraphism earlier/later.

general: All mechanics requires shields! | ➔ Indicates Carry Over

footnotes.1: Provide single target mitigation and GCD shield both tanks in the phase transition for Ultimate Embrace. Also assist tanks with the last Ultimate Embrace.
footnotes.2: Prepare Spreadlo either on the OT shortly beforehand or the MT during Ultimate Embrace to assist the tanks.
footnotes.3: Use Holos during the first Ultimate Embrace so it is back for Light of Judgement and provides mitigation to the tanks. Alternatively, you can use Holos for the Wings of Destruction + Ultimate Embrace.
footnotes.4: Use early to avoid shaking off mitigation if playing WAR.

mechanics.name: Accretions
mechanics.note:
Healer Accretion 1st | DPS Accretion 2nd
Tanks and DPS should not use any abilities that boosts HP such as Second Wind and Curing Waltz.

general: All mechanics requires shields! | ➔ Indicates Carry Over

Targeted mitigation must be on your firewalled target unless the firewall is down. For the most part, most targeted mitigation is mostly filler and does not work on raidwides. It is mainly used for minimizing tank autos and/or busters.
Both tanks will get attacked for moderately high damage throughout the entire phase, ensure you are rolling mitigation and heals on them.

footnotes.1: At the beginning of the phase, use 30s mitigation after (when the textbox disappears) Kefka says, "Oh! What other toys can I throw in here..." to get tank autos and the raidwide + an additional usage for Stray Flames/Tsunami.
footnotes.2: There is a very small period where you can cover both hits of Thunder III and the next Stray Flames/Tsumani, if you miss the timing, you can use it next GCD.
footnotes.3: Use if holding Chaos, otherwise use at the beginning of P4 for autos.
footnotes.4:
Non-healers should avoid using any healing abilities that may cause their Accretion to pop early such as Second Wind, Curing Waltz or Divine Veil. If both Accretions are activated in a short amount of time, it will cause a wipe.
Healers will need to manage HP burst accordingly to ensure that Accretions are not popped together. The H1 and H2 can throw single target heals at whoever has the Accretion between them.
If playing AST, ensure the vulnerability has expired before popping Macrocosmos. WHM can use Benediction (if not used earlier) to instantly pop the healer Accretion.
footnotes.5: If you are holding Exdeath instead of Chaos at the beginning, use Reprisal on both before The Decisive Battle finishes.
footnotes.6: Use LB3 at the W of Vacuum Wave. Either tank can press it, discuss beforehand.
footnotes.7: Seraphism can be shifted to P4 if you feel you have sufficient mitigation.
footnotes.8: Prepare Spreadlo on the tanks, prioritizing WAR > DRK > GNB/PLD.
footnotes.9: Prepare immediately after Bowels of Agony.
footnotes.10: Both healers should have instant cast heals prepared to top up the party between the first and second set of stomps.

general: All mechanics requires shields! | ➔ Indicates Carry Over
Targeted mitigation (Reprisal, Addle, etc) only works on Ultima Upsurge, the rest is used to assist in mitigating tank auto attacks.

footnotes.1: Use at the beginning of the phase for autos.

mechanics.name: Forsaken
mechanics.note:
For Forsaken, use any timed mitigation as late as possible unless otherwise noted.
It is important that the new round of mitigation for the 5th hit are applied as the first round of mitigation will fall off.

general: All mechanics requires shields! | ➔ Indicates Carry Over
For Forsaken, use any timed mitigation as late as possible unless otherwise noted.

footnotes.1: Use when Kefka brings his staff down to his right side, example here. | The subsequent usages should be pressed immediately off cooldown.
footnotes.2: Healers should monitor the tanks during Maddening Orchestra (especially the Flare tank) and Fell Forces. For WAR/DRK, you will need to have single target burst healing prepared after their invulnerability expires so they can survive the 3rd auto.
footnotes.3: Use two GCDs after the Stray Apocalypse castbar is completed so it is back for Forsaken.
footnotes.5: Use after the third towers in Celestriad resolves.

footnotes.1: Revolting Ruin III targets first in enmity and retargets to second in emity when the castbar ends.
footnotes.2: Hyperdrive does not have a castbar and occurs immediately after Light of Judgement.
footnotes.3: WAR can alternatively kitchen sink this and Holmgang the first Ultimate Embrace, let your party know if you plan to do this.

footnotes.1: Rampart end of first Wings of Destruction (halfroom) cast, 40% two GCDs later and 10s mitigation at the end of second Wings of Destruction cast.
footnotes.2: Short mitigation before Ultimate Embrace finishes casting.
footnotes.3: PLD can take the Wings of Destruction at the wall to assist their co-tank with no downtime (discuss beforehand if MT).

general: Regardless of who is MT or OT, the invulnerability priority overrides this. You will still mitigate (Reprisal & Party Mit) based on whether you are MT or OT on the main page. This is for middle Exdeath only.
Tanks should use their short mitigation for the autos throughout the phase (WAR use Nascent).
Kefka does not do any busters throughout the phase, but he does continously auto the main threat dealing around 75k without any mitigation. Ensure CDs are available for P5.

sections.title: Phase 5: Ultima Kefka
columns: PLD > GNB > DRK > WAR (Start With Boss) ¹

PLD > GNB > DRK > WAR (Start With Boss).raw: Rampart ⁵ + 40% + Short Mit (PLD/DRK) + Provoke ²

general: Regardless of who is MT or OT, the invulnerability priority overrides this. You will still mitigate (Reprisal & Party Mit) based on whether you are MT or OT on the main page.

footnotes.1: The tank who is not invulning first must have main threat to not get Holy.
footnotes.2: The invulning tank should Provoke as soon Flare/Holy is applied so Kefka is not pulled and they get main threat for the autos.
footnotes.3: The third auto must be shared as invulnerabilities will run out.
footnotes.4: Invulnerabilities should be used just before the Flare/Holy debuffs expire so that they can cover two autos.
footnotes.5: Rampart shoud be pressed just before the Maddening Orchestra cast is complete so it carries over to the 3rd auto.
